summary: Engineered scalable cloud-native backend systems and modernized enterprise frontend applications.
description: I have engineered highly scalable, cloud-native backend systems, taking ownership of critical publishing pipelines and event-driven architectures. By designing horizontally scalable data streaming with Kafka and implementing dynamic deployment toggles, I successfully reduced the load on legacy infrastructure while significantly decoupling complex architectural paths. My work also involved integrating high-throughput network modules and migrating monolithic logic to microservices-based approaches—supporting huge request volumes with negligible latency overhead. Additionally, I architected various optimization workflows, including threshold-breach notification systems that minimized redundant messaging, and implemented secure, backward-compatible deployment mechanisms. On the frontend, I modernized legacy dashboards using Next.js and a modular component-based design, measurably improving both load speeds and user satisfaction. Finally, I optimized automated file processing with resilient distributed caching, cutting down manual intervention by orders of magnitude. Working across the full development life-cycle, I've driven impactful feature delivery while actively resolving critical production incidents to maintain consistent high availability.

summary: Spearheaded core Java microservice migrations, improved testing workflows, and scaled deployments.
description: During my internship, I took ownership of modernizing core application dependencies, notably driving a major Java version migration strategy across the team. By meticulously resolving dependency conflicts, I eliminated critical security vulnerabilities and ensured compliance. I also developed custom build utilities that simplified dependency management and accelerated the development lifecycle by significantly reducing build times. Recognizing the importance of reliability, I championed testing by introducing robust unit tests for newly decoupled microservices using advanced testing frameworks, resulting in a dramatic increase in overall code coverage. I actively participated in infrastructure and operations by executing hybrid deployments across On-Premises and Kubernetes environments leveraging Helm charts. Through load testing and performance validation, I ensured that high-volume processor services maintained strict latency requirements at scale. This internship solidified my foundations in microservices deployment, infrastructure management, and maintaining code quality in complex enterprise systems.

summary: Optimized real-time collaborative text-editor features and developed responsive UI web components.
description: At Microsoft, I worked on enhancing real-time collaborative environments, directly contributing to core features that required highly responsive user experiences. My primary focus was on resolving latency issues and restoring critical text-editor functionality that had been impacted by system regressions. I efficiently debugged and unblocked these technical dependencies, bringing back complete text-editing capabilities to the platform. Furthermore, I expanded the capabilities of various UI components, such as introducing custom schema support for user-selection modules, which significantly reduced execution overhead. This experience exposed me to the intricacies of building highly responsive frontends and working within large-scale TypeScript and React codebases, emphasizing the importance of latency optimization and seamless user-centric design.
